ArmorClassTypes.brief: Constants defining various armor class categories for damage type identification and mitigation.
ArmorClassTypes.details: The ArmorClassTypes group encapsulates constants that categorize armor based on damage types such as piercing, bashing, slashing, and exotic. These identifiers are integral to the game's damage calculation system, enabling precise and consistent handling of damage mitigation, resistance, and display logic. By organizing these constants within a dedicated group, the codebase ensures clarity, maintainability, and ease of extension when supporting additional damage types or armor categories. They serve as standardized references used throughout combat mechanics, UI representations, and damage processing routines.
ArmorClassTypes.notes: The constants follow a sequential integer assignment, facilitating efficient comparisons and storage. They are intended to be used as fixed identifiers across the system, ensuring uniform handling of damage interactions related to armor classes.
ArmorClassTypes.rationale: This group exists to provide a clear and maintainable way to categorize armor based on damage types, supporting consistent damage mitigation logic and user interface displays. It helps separate damage type logic from other game mechanics, promoting modularity and ease of updates in the combat system.
ArmorClassTypes.name: ArmorClassTypes

AC_PIERCE.brief: Constant representing the armor class category for piercing damage types.
AC_PIERCE.details: AC_PIERCE is a constant integer used to identify the armor class category associated with piercing damage within the game mechanics. Initialized to zero, it serves as a standardized identifier across multiple functions, such as damage calculations, status displays, and armor assessments, ensuring consistent handling of piercing damage interactions. Its usage simplifies referencing the specific armor class type related to piercing attacks, facilitating accurate damage mitigation and display logic.
AC_PIERCE.notes: This constant is typically used as a parameter in functions that retrieve or display armor class information related to piercing damage, promoting code clarity and consistency.
AC_PIERCE.rationale: The constant exists to provide a clear, unambiguous identifier for the piercing damage armor class category, enabling uniform handling of piercing damage interactions and ensuring that related calculations and displays are correctly associated with this damage type.
AC_PIERCE.name: AC_PIERCE

AC_BASH.brief: Constant representing the armor class category specifically for bashing damage types.
AC_BASH.details: The `AC_BASH` constant serves as an identifier for the armor class value associated with bashing attacks in the game's combat mechanics. Initialized to 1, it is used across multiple functions to retrieve, display, and modify a character's armor class against bashing damage, such as in `GET_AC`, `do_bash`, and display routines. This categorization enables the system to handle different damage types distinctly, ensuring accurate calculations and representations of a character's defensive capabilities against bashing attacks.
AC_BASH.notes: The constant is stored as an integer value and is part of a set of armor class type identifiers, facilitating clear and consistent referencing throughout the codebase.
AC_BASH.rationale: This constant exists to provide a clear, maintainable way to identify and work with the armor class value for bashing damage, supporting accurate combat interactions and damage calculations involving bashing attacks.
AC_BASH.name: AC_BASH

AC_SLASH.brief: Constant that identifies the armor class category for slashing damage types.
AC_SLASH.details: The constant AC_SLASH, initialized to 2, serves as a standardized identifier for the slashing damage armor class within the codebase. It is used in functions that retrieve, display, or calculate a character's defense against slashing attacks, such as GET_AC and various UI routines. By using this constant, the code maintains consistency when referencing the slashing armor class across different modules, facilitating easier maintenance and clearer understanding of damage mitigation mechanics.
AC_SLASH.notes: This constant is typically used as an argument in functions that handle armor class calculations and display, ensuring that slashing defense values are correctly identified and processed.
AC_SLASH.rationale: AC_SLASH exists to provide a clear, maintainable way to refer to the slashing armor class category, supporting accurate damage mitigation calculations and consistent user interface displays related to slashing defense.
AC_SLASH.name: AC_SLASH

AC_EXOTIC.brief: Constant representing the armor class category for exotic damage types.
AC_EXOTIC.details: The constant `AC_EXOTIC` is used to identify and categorize armor class values specifically against exotic damage types within the game. It is initialized to the value 3 and is referenced across various functions and classes to retrieve, display, and initialize the victim’s armor class against exotic attacks. This constant facilitates consistent handling of exotic damage resistance and ensures that related calculations, displays, and data storage are aligned with this specific armor class category, enabling accurate damage mitigation and status reporting for exotic damage types.
AC_EXOTIC.notes: This constant is typically used as an argument in functions that handle armor class calculations and display, ensuring uniformity in referencing the exotic damage category throughout the codebase.
AC_EXOTIC.rationale: The `AC_EXOTIC` constant exists to provide a clear, standardized identifier for exotic damage armor class calculations, supporting consistent damage resistance mechanics and user interface displays related to exotic damage types.
AC_EXOTIC.name: AC_EXOTIC
